object_identification.name: blue pen
object_identification.attributes: Small cylindrical writing instrument with a blue plastic body, tapered tip, and capped or retractable pen form; lightweight, narrow, and easy to roll on a flat surface

grasp.contact_zone: Mid-body barrel area away from the tip and clip, using thumb and index finger with optional middle finger support
grasp.accessibility: Highly accessible on an open tabletop; unobstructed approach from above, though its slim shape requires careful alignment to avoid slipping or pushing it away

planning.approach: Move the end effector above the pen centerline, align with the long axis, and descend slowly while compensating for any small roll or pose change
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Gripper jaws parallel to the pen axis and slightly open, with the approach vector vertical or slightly angled to match table clearance
planning.grasp_contact: Close fingertips gently on the barrel at the center region, avoiding the tip, clip, and cap boundary; apply only enough force to prevent rolling
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift vertically a short distance, then maintain a level orientation and minimal grip force to keep the pen from rotating or slipping

use.description: Writing or note-taking on paper or a whiteboard
use.link_to_grasp: The precision grasp positions the pen for controlled tip contact and fine motion during writing
use.physical_constraints: Requires stable tip exposure, light grip force, and enough dexterity to orient the tip at the correct writing angle

use.description: Marking, labeling, or drawing simple annotations
use.link_to_grasp: The mid-barrel grasp supports quick repositioning and accurate short strokes for marking tasks
use.physical_constraints: Needs low slip, adequate tip pressure, and controlled wrist motion to avoid smudging or skipping

robustness.assumptions: The object is a standard lightweight plastic pen resting on a flat table and is not stuck, damaged, or partially occluded
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If pose is uncertain, first center the gripper over the visible barrel, use visual servoing to refine alignment, and close with compliant force control to prevent pushing the pen away

object_identification.name: blue ink gel pen
object_identification.attributes: cylindrical plastic body with metal clip, textured rubber grip, hydrophobic smooth surface, lightweight

grasp.contact_zone: mid-section cylindrical body near textured grip
grasp.accessibility: high, full 360-degree visibility, no occlusions

planning.approach: vertical approach along the pen's longitudinal axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper fingers parallel to pen's length with slight downward tilt
planning.grasp_contact: squeeze center of the pen while keeping tip clearance from gripper jaws
planning.lift_stabilization: maintain axial alignment during lift to prevent torque-induced spin

use.description: ink writing on paper
use.link_to_grasp: grasp tip-inverted for downward force vector during writing stroke
use.physical_constraints: requires fine motor control and constant tip pressure modulation

use.description: transfer to desk organizer
use.link_to_grasp: maintain mid-body pinch before insertion into ambient air slot
use.physical_constraints: requires accurate depth insertion to avoid dropping or jamming

robustness.assumptions: object is rigid enough to be gripped without deforming, no tip damage exists
robustness.uncertainty_handling: verify object height before contact to avoid pixelation errors at tip and cap

object_identification.name: blue retractable ballpoint pen
object_identification.attributes: cylindrical, small diameter, smooth plastic body with rubber grip section, blue ink indicator on barrel, white text on barrel

grasp.contact_zone: two opposing fingers contact the cylindrical barrel, ideally around the central center of gravity to minimize rotational torque
grasp.accessibility: perfect accessibility from a top-down approach as the pen lies flat on an unobstructed table surface

planning.approach: descend standard 2-finger gripper vertically from directly above the pen center
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: orient gripper pads to align parallel with the pen's longitudinal axis
planning.grasp_contact: lowering gripper slowly until the pen barrel is positioned high within the finger pads before closing fingers with gentle, steady force
planning.lift_stabilization: apply uniform grasping force, then execute a slow, smooth vertical lift to avoid slippage or induced swinging

use.description: writing or drawing on a surface
use.link_to_grasp: transition from a 2-finger pinch grasp to a broader, more human-like tripodal grip using a multi-fingered robotic hand for stability and control on the pen's body
use.physical_constraints: high precision required for tip positioning, contact force sensing for ink flow, high coefficient of friction for non-slip grip

use.description: re-orienting the object after movement or picking up
use.link_to_grasp: rotate the pen along its main axis or change the direction it faces after moving it to a new location, often to hand the pen off to another system
use.physical_constraints: ensure that the current grasp can achieve the desired orientation without slipping or needing to be re-positioned, potentially using re-grasping

robustness.assumptions: the pen's geometry is consistent with standard model parameters, and the table surface is frictionless enough for micro-slippage without shifting the pen significantly
robustness.uncertainty_handling: use vision-based localization to correct for pen position deviations before grasp; implement force feedback during lifting to detect potential slippage, triggering a prompt decrease in acceleration

object_identification.name: gel roller ball pen
object_identification.attributes: smooth cylindrical body (5-7cm length), blue plastic cap, metallic tip, lightweight (~5-10g), reflective surface, slightly curved tip

grasp.contact_zone: midshaft (avoiding tip/cap) with fingertips and thumb pad
grasp.accessibility: high (exposed, no obstructions)

planning.approach: vertical alignment from above at 15-30 degree angle
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: pen tip angled slightly downward (30 degrees from horizontal)
planning.grasp_contact: thumb contacts cap side, index/finger contacts body 2-3cm from tip
planning.lift_stabilization: controlled upward force (0.3-0.5N) with rotational stabilization

use.description: writing annotations on whiteboards or paper documents
use.link_to_grasp: stable grasp prevents roll during writing
use.physical_constraints: cap must remain closed; smooth writing surface required

use.description: marking labels or outlines on wooden surfaces (e.g., drafting)
use.link_to_grasp: precise orientation ensures consistent line width
use.physical_constraints: pen tip must be clean; surface must be flat and uncluttered

robustness.assumptions: pen is dry, cap is closed, no external obstructions, lighting allows RGB-D detection
robustness.uncertainty_handling: adaptive force control for tip slippage; visual servoing for alignment correction

object_identification.name: blue retractable ballpoint pen
object_identification.attributes: glossy blue plastic cylindrical body, retractable click mechanism, small lightweight office writing instrument, smooth exterior, standard 14cm length, 1cm diameter

grasp.contact_zone: midsection of the pen's body, 1cm away from both the top clicker end and the bottom writing tip, held between the gripper's thumb and index finger pads
grasp.accessibility: fully accessible on an open flat table surface, no surrounding obstacles, approachable from any angle above the tabletop

planning.approach: descend vertically from 15cm above the table towards the pen's midsection, align the gripper's pinch axis parallel to the pen's long axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper jaws open to 2cm width, positioned directly above the pen's midpoint to evenly span the pen's diameter
planning.grasp_contact: slowly close gripper jaws to apply a consistent 5N force on the pen's midsection, securing the pen without deforming its plastic body
planning.lift_stabilization: lift the pen 10cm vertically above the table, pause for 0.5 seconds to confirm no slippage or tilting before moving to the target use location

use.description: Write handwritten notes, marks or drawings on flat paper surfaces for office or personal use
use.link_to_grasp: retain the same precision pinch grasp, adjust the pen's angle to 45 degrees relative to the paper surface, apply 2N additional force to the clicker end to extend the writing tip
use.physical_constraints: Requires a flat porous paper surface, pen must be angled 30-60 degrees to write cleanly, excess force will cause ink smudging

use.description: Make temporary legible marks on non-porous office whiteboards for meetings or presentations
use.link_to_grasp: keep the core precision pinch grasp, adjust the pen's orientation to 50 degrees relative to the vertical whiteboard, activate the retractable tip to begin marking
use.physical_constraints: Requires a smooth non-porous whiteboard surface, maintain light consistent pressure to avoid ink skipping or smudging, cannot apply force exceeding 3N to the whiteboard surface

robustness.assumptions: The pen is rigid with a uniform smooth exterior, the table surface is flat and stable, the robot's gripper can detect applied force to avoid damage, no objects obscure the pen's position
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If initial grasp slips, reposition the gripper 0.5cm from the original midsection, re-close jaws with 7N force; if the pen tilts after lifting, lower it back to the table, re-orient the gripper and re-grasp for stability

object_identification.name: grey ceramic mug
object_identification.attributes: Tall cylindrical mug with a single side handle, glossy grey exterior, open top, and a small amount of liquid residue visible inside; placed upright on a flat wooden table.

grasp.contact_zone: Primary contact on the handle, with optional light stabilizing contact near the mug body only after secure handle engagement
grasp.accessibility: Handle is clearly exposed and accessible from the left side of the mug, making it easy for a robot gripper or two-finger hand to approach without obstruction.

planning.approach: Approach from the side of the handle with the gripper aligned to enter the handle opening laterally, keeping the mug body clear of collision.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Orient the gripper fingers parallel to the handle opening, slightly open wider than the handle thickness, with wrist level to the table surface.
planning.grasp_contact: Close fingers around the handle until firm contact is made on opposite sides of the handle; avoid squeezing the mug body to reduce slip or breakage risk.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift vertically a short distance, then hold the mug level and stabilize with gentle force while monitoring for slosh due to the liquid inside.

use.description: Drink service or beverage transport
use.link_to_grasp: The handle grasp supports controlled lifting and carrying of the mug while keeping the contents relatively stable.
use.physical_constraints: Keep the mug upright to prevent spilling; limit acceleration and tilting because the mug may contain liquid.

use.description: Move the mug to a sink, tray, or dishwasher for cleaning
use.link_to_grasp: A secure handle grasp allows the robot to relocate the mug without contacting the rim or interior.
use.physical_constraints: Avoid impact with nearby objects and maintain vertical orientation if liquid remains inside; handle and mug should not be overloaded.

robustness.assumptions: Assume the mug is rigid, lightweight, and graspable via a standard side handle; assume the table is stable and the mug is not stuck to the surface.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If the handle pose is uncertain, re-estimate from multiple views and perform a slower pre-grasp alignment; if liquid slosh is detected, reduce speed and keep the wrist level during motion.

object_identification.name: grey ceramic mug
object_identification.attributes: moderate capacity, cylindrical with handle, dark grey matte finish, contains warm liquid with visible foam

grasp.contact_zone: mug handle and lateral walls at approximately 8-10 o'clock positions
grasp.accessibility: highly accessible for robotic grippers approaching from the side

planning.approach: vertical approach from the side of the mug to avoid interference with the handle, maintain a 5 cm proximity before contact
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper fingers aligned parallel to the table surface, feet positioned near the mug's upper third and middle
planning.grasp_contact: pinch the handle and the alternating opposite lateral wall to prevent rotation during lift
planning.lift_stabilization: apply upward force at the handle primary to lift the mug with counter-rotation moment to neutralize tipping

use.description: drinking hot beverage
use.link_to_grasp: secured handle grip allows controlled elevation for bringing to a mouth-sized opening
use.physical_constraints: must lift vertically without tilting the mug beyond 30 degrees to prevent spilling the foam

use.description: transporting on tray or caddy
use.link_to_grasp: side grasp provides stability for placing inside a secondary container
use.physical_constraints: container height must accommodate mug plus an additional 1 cm for cushioning

robustness.assumptions: stable base, porous handle for grip, no lateral deformations in the mug structure
robustness.uncertainty_handling: sensor-based force feedback to detect slipping or misalignment during approach, and adaptive force compensation during lift

object_identification.name: dark gray ceramic mug
object_identification.attributes: dark gray color, cylindrical main body with a wider rim, single C-shaped handle, currently resting with the mouth facing up, containing residual liquid/powder, smooth glazed surface

grasp.type: top-down cylindrical power grasp or handle-integrated grasp
grasp.contact_zone: The outer surface of the mug's main body, or the handle for higher-friction manipulation. The body offers a larger, more reliable contact area for a standard parallel-jaw gripper.
grasp.accessibility: High. The object has significant clearance around it on the tabletop, with no immediate obstacles within the robotic arm's primary working volume. The rim and the top part of the body are fully exposed.

planning.approach: vertical top-down approach path for a grasp on the main body, ensuring alignment with the mug's central vertical axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper jaws spread to a width wider than the mug's body. Fingers positioned at 12 and 6 o'clock relative to the mug if grasping the body, or 12 and 3 o'clock if grasping the body with minimal handle interference.
planning.grasp_contact: lowering the gripper until the fingertips are at the mug's vertical midpoint. Closing the jaws linearly to make simultaneous contact on opposite sides of the ceramic body, applying controlled force.
planning.lift_stabilization: A controlled vertical lift to ensure friction between the gripper and the mug is sufficient. The system assumes a minimum mass and high friction to prevent slipping. Stability is critical due to potential liquid content. Use of force/torque sensors for feedback.

use.description: pour contents of mug into another container
use.link_to_grasp: requires a secure, non-slip grasp to handle the dynamic shifting center of gravity as the liquid pours
use.physical_constraints: needs high precision reorientation (rotation on horizontal axis) and a robust grasp with minimal slippage to avoid spills

use.description: transport the mug to a different location (e.g., a dishwashing station)
use.link_to_grasp: a cylindrical power grasp on the body is generally more robust for long-distance transport than a grasp on the handle alone
use.physical_constraints: low-acceleration movement is necessary to prevent sloshing of residual liquid. Requires reliable position control and obstacle avoidance.

robustness.assumptions: The mug is ceramic, and its surface is dry and relatively clean. The residual pattern inside is just powder and poses no significant contamination risk to the sensor or surface.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Use depth information to plan to grasp slightly below the center of mass. Implement slip detection using force/torque sensors. Use tactile feedback if available to adapt grip force without crushing the object.

object_identification.name: grey ceramic mug with handle
object_identification.attributes: smooth rounded surface, cylindrical hollow body, thin ceramic material, dark grey color, exterior handle for left-hand grasping, no visible texture repetitions beyond minor manufacturing striations

grasp.contact_zone: outer ring around openings and handle hub
grasp.accessibility: medium (visible, upright, slightly protruding from table edge)

planning.approach: lateral horizontal c-plane plan from the right side at 30° downward angle toward handle
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: -X-axis perpendicular to handle base, Y-axis parallel to handle curvature, -Z-axis downward toward contact surface
planning.grasp_contact: soft paralleljaw grip applying slight detent pressure to handle hub and ring base contact
planning.lift_stabilization: carry trajectory modulating downward inverse dynamics while opposed friction surfaces minimize rotational slip

use.description: hot liquid dispensing container (preheated or cooled beverages)
use.link_to_grasp: handle-based orientation allows ergonomic tipping without grasp reconfiguration
use.physical_constraints: limiting max internal pressure to ceramic flexure threshold (F_max=45 N/mm), μ_surface/posture-dependent tipping angle <25°

use.description: office supply storage (dry items like pens or discs for small office largesse)
use.link_to_grasp: base-down precarious grasp transitions to horizontal carrier pose via rotational ascent matrix transform
use.physical_constraints: stability ensured within COG displacement <8 mm from base centroid, achieving a floor distance minimization for load balancing

robustness.assumptions: static non-viscous frictional contact assumptions for calcium-alumina surfaces, assumed centroid (CX=CY=0) for open-mouth base point localization with SD≤2mm, table coplanarity within ±3°
robustness.uncertainty_handling: SLAM-tolerant grip controller prioritizes handle-based recognize-actuate-feedback cycle at 16Hz; implements uncertainty propagation via Kalman smoothing for hybrid/soft grip inversion path planning

object_identification.name: grey ceramic mug
object_identification.attributes: matte grey exterior, C-shaped side handle, tapered cylindrical body, contains residual light-brown beverage, smooth ceramic surface, placed on an unobstructed wooden tabletop

grasp.contact_zone: inner and outer surfaces of the mug's C-shaped handle, with no contact with the mug's open rim to avoid spillage or contamination
grasp.accessibility: the mug is fully accessible, with no obstacles blocking approach to its handle or body, positioned on an open, flat table surface

planning.approach: move the robotic gripper toward the mug's handle from the side, aligning the gripper's jaws to slot around the handle
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: orient the gripper horizontally parallel to the tabletop, with jaws aligned to wrap around the handle without contacting the main cup body
planning.grasp_contact: close the gripper jaws to apply even, gentle pressure on the handle, securing a stable hold without exerting enough force to damage the ceramic material
planning.lift_stabilization: lift the mug straight up 10cm above the table first, maintain a vertical orientation to prevent spilling residual beverage, then move the mug to its target location

use.description: Transport the used mug to a sink for washing and cleaning
use.link_to_grasp: retain the handle grasp throughout transport, keep the mug level to avoid spilling leftover contents
use.physical_constraints: cannot tilt the mug more than 5 degrees from vertical, the gripper must support the mug's total weight of approximately 0.5kg

use.description: Position the mug under a coffee dispenser to refill with a fresh hot beverage
use.link_to_grasp: maintain the handle grasp to position the mug under the dispenser spout, adjust orientation only slightly to align for pouring, return to level after dispensing
use.physical_constraints: keep the gripper at least 15cm away from hot dispenser components to avoid heat damage, center the mug under the spout to prevent spillage during refilling

robustness.assumptions: the mug's handle is structurally intact to support its weight, the mug is stationary on the flat table, no hidden obstacles block approach paths
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if the handle is not detected, switch to a side pinch grasp on the mug's lower cylindrical body to lift it; use visual feedback to adjust grasp position if the mug's orientation shifts during approach

object_identification.name: computer mouse
object_identification.attributes: small black wired optical mouse with a red scroll wheel/button area, Lenovo branding, smooth curved top shell, and a USB cable attached

grasp.type: top pinch grasp with side support
grasp.contact_zone: upper shell on the left and right sides of the mouse body, avoiding the scroll wheel and cable strain area
grasp.accessibility: high; the mouse is isolated on a flat table and can be approached from above with minimal obstruction, though the cable should be managed to prevent tugging

planning.approach: move the gripper from above and slightly behind the mouse, align with the mouse's long axis, and avoid contacting the cable first
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: parallel to the table surface with the gripper opening wider than the mouse width, centered over the middle of the shell
planning.grasp_contact: close the fingers gently on both side surfaces of the mouse body, applying enough force to secure the smooth plastic without pressing the buttons or scroll wheel
planning.lift_stabilization: lift vertically a short distance while monitoring cable slack, then adjust grip if the mouse tilts or the cable pulls

use.description: control a computer pointer for desktop interaction
use.link_to_grasp: the robot lifts and places the mouse so a human or another system can use it on a desk
use.physical_constraints: must keep the bottom sensor unobstructed and preserve free cable movement for normal operation

use.description: transport the mouse to a storage or docking location
use.link_to_grasp: a secure side grasp allows the robot to relocate the device without dragging the cable
use.physical_constraints: avoid excessive squeezing, bending the cable near the connector, or dropping the lightweight plastic body

robustness.assumptions: the mouse is lightweight, rigid, and not powered by a heavy battery; the cable is flexible and can be safely moved aside
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if the cable is tangled, first clear slack near the body; if the exact center of mass is uncertain, use a slightly wider grasp and low force, then regrasp after lift if needed

object_identification.name: computer mouse
object_identification.attributes: black, wired, lenovo brand, optical sensor, USB cable attached, red scroll wheel button

grasp.contact_zone: left or right side surface of the mouse body
grasp.accessibility: high

planning.approach: approach from the side parallel to the table surface to avoid snagging the cable
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: fingers parallel to the longitudinal axis of the mouse
planning.grasp_contact: fingers close around the side surface distal to the cable exit
planning.lift_stabilization: lift vertically while ensuring no torque is applied to the cable

use.description: navigation on a computer screen by moving the mouse
use.link_to_grasp: lateral grip allows smooth movement without tilting the device excessively
use.physical_constraints: must ensure the cable has slack to prevent pulling the mouse

use.description: selecting items by clicking the buttons
use.link_to_grasp: stable side grip keeps the sensor flat on the surface
use.physical_constraints: thumb must be accessible to press the scroll wheel or side buttons

robustness.assumptions: the mouse is not connected to a live system and the table surface is flat and non-slippery
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if cable entanglement is detected during approach, retract reposition the cable before grasping

object_identification.name: Wired Computer Mouse
object_identification.attributes: Small, ergonomic, palm-sized, lightweight, plastic material, symmetric design, USB-A cable attached, black color with red scroll wheel.

grasp.type: 2-finger parallel jaw or multi-finger encompassing grasp
grasp.contact_zone: Sides of the mouse body (dominant approach) or encompassing top/bottom.
grasp.accessibility: High. The object is isolated on a flat surface, offering ample space for a parallel jaw gripper to approach from the sides.

planning.approach: Vertical approach from above the object.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Rotate the end-effector to align the gripper jaws parallel to the long axis of the mouse body. Ensure sufficient jaw opening.
planning.grasp_contact: Lower the end-effector. Apply clamping force to the sides of the mouse. Maintain slight downward pressure to avoid slippage during closing.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift slowly to verify a secure grasp. Compensate for the center of gravity if grasping away from the mouse's midpoint.

use.description: Operating the PC interface.
use.link_to_grasp: This requires making specific fingertip contacts on the mouse buttons (left/right primary buttons) and engaging the scroll wheel. A standard side-grasp is not suitable; this requires a precise fingertip or pulp-to-pulp grasp on the buttons.
use.physical_constraints: High dexterity needed to exert downward force on single buttons without shifting the mouse body. The surface must be non-slip (e.g., a mousepad) for the sensor to work.

use.description: Moving and repositioning the mouse.
use.link_to_grasp: A robust encompassing grasp to lift and relocate the object on the desk.
use.physical_constraints: Low constraints; simple pick-and-place operation.

robustness.assumptions: Assumes the object's mass is centered and minimal friction on the surface doesn't cause excessive sliding during jaw closure.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Incorporate force feedback to detect contact and ensure the gripper is not bottoming out on the table before lifting. Use slip detection sensors on the finger pads.

object_identification.name: computer_mouse
object_identification.attributes: wired, ergonomic, USB connector, smooth surface, curved body, red left-button indicator

grasp.contact_zone: side_and_bottom_flat_surface
grasp.accessibility: moderate_due_to_cable_constraints

planning.approach: top_down_alignment_with_cable_avoidance
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: upright_90_degrees_to_cable_direction
planning.grasp_contact: fingers_on_side_and_thumb_on_bottom_edge
planning.lift_stabilization: gentle_lift_with_cable_tension_control

use.description: input_device_for_computer_cursor_control
use.link_to_grasp: stable_grasp_ensures_precise_placement_on_desktop
use.physical_constraints: clear_workspace_of_obstacles_and_ensure_cable_path_is_unobstructed

use.description: storage_transport_of_small_tech_items
use.link_to_grasp: secure_holding_for_transport_with_cable_tucked_in
use.physical_constraints: use_soft_container_to_protect_surface_and_cable

robustness.assumptions: mouse_surface_is_dry_and_free_of_debris, cable_is_not_tangled_or_fractured
robustness.uncertainty_handling: adaptive_grip_force_with_force_torque_sensing, visual_cable_obstacle_avoidance

object_identification.name: Lenovo wired USB computer mouse
object_identification.attributes: black matte plastic body, red accent scroll wheel, attached black USB-A cable, standard compact desktop mouse form factor, optical tracking sensor on its base

grasp.contact_zone: left and right smooth lateral side surfaces of the mouse, avoiding the top primary buttons and scroll wheel to prevent component damage
grasp.accessibility: fully unobstructed, positioned on a clear table allowing approach from all angles

planning.approach: end effector approaches from a top-down direction, aligned with the mouse's long axis to avoid tangling with the attached cable
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper fingers spaced slightly wider than the mouse's width, oriented parallel to the mouse's lateral edges to prepare for secure grasping
planning.grasp_contact: gripper closes gently to apply even low force on the mouse's side surfaces, securing the mouse without compressing its internal components
planning.lift_stabilization: lift the mouse slowly 10-15cm above the table, maintaining a level orientation to prevent the attached cable from snagging on the table surface during movement

use.description: Operate as a computer input device to navigate a cursor on a connected desktop or laptop
use.link_to_grasp: the same lateral grasp is used to place the mouse on a compatible mousepad, then the gripper releases to enable functional use; the robot can also maintain the grasp to move the mouse across the surface to control the cursor directly
use.physical_constraints: must be placed on a flat, non-transparent surface for the optical sensor to function, USB cable must be plugged into a powered computer USB port to transmit input and receive power

use.description: Relocate the mouse to a desktop storage tray to clear workspace clutter
use.link_to_grasp: the lateral pinch grasp remains consistent for lifting, carrying, and placing the mouse into the storage tray, providing enough grip strength to prevent drops during transport
use.physical_constraints: storage space must be large enough to fit the mouse and its coiled cable, the storage surface must support the mouse's ~80g weight to avoid falls

robustness.assumptions: the mouse is stationary, its side surfaces are free of slippery debris, the cable is not tangled with any other objects on the table
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if initial position detection is off, the gripper performs a small 2cm lateral adjustment to align with the mouse's sides before closing; if the cable snags during lifting, the robot lowers the mouse back to the table, reorients to untangle the cable, and retries the lift

object_identification.name: orange balloon
object_identification.attributes: Small deflated latex balloon, orange color, lightweight, flexible, thin-walled, with a visible opening at the neck and a wrinkled flattened body

grasp.type: precision pinch grasp at the neck/opening
grasp.contact_zone: The narrow neck near the rolled opening, avoiding the thin inflated body area
grasp.accessibility: High; the balloon lies on a flat table and the neck is exposed, making it easy to approach from above

planning.approach: Move the gripper above the balloon with slow vertical descent and center the fingers around the neck region
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Align the gripper opening parallel to the table surface and perpendicular to the balloon neck axis for a stable pinch
planning.grasp_contact: Close the fingertips gently on the neck and lip of the opening, applying minimal force to avoid tearing or slipping
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift slowly with reduced acceleration and keep the balloon suspended with low gripping pressure to prevent deformation or dropping

use.description: Inflate it for a party decoration or simple visual prop
use.link_to_grasp: The robot can pick it up by the neck and hand it to an inflator or move it to a display area
use.physical_constraints: Requires very gentle handling because latex can stretch or tear easily

use.description: Use it as a lightweight demo object for robot manipulation testing
use.link_to_grasp: A pinch grasp on the neck is suitable for transfer, placement, or sorting demonstrations
use.physical_constraints: Must avoid sharp tools, excessive squeeze force, and rough surfaces that could damage the balloon

robustness.assumptions: The object is a standard latex balloon and is not stuck to the surface or tangled with other items
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If the neck position is unclear, the robot should re-estimate the opening location, use a low-force compliant grasp, and retry with a slightly offset pinch if slippage is detected

object_identification.name: uninflated latex balloon
object_identification.attributes: orange, soft, flexible, toroidal shape with a small neck

grasp.contact_zone: neck/nozzle
grasp.accessibility: highly accessible distinct feature

planning.approach: vertical downward approach
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper aligned with neck axis
planning.grasp_contact: pinch the rim of the nozzle
planning.lift_stabilization: lift straight up to prevent slippage

use.description: Inflation of the balloon
use.link_to_grasp: grasping the neck allows for inflation without stretching the finger points
use.physical_constraints: requires keeping the nozzle pinched to hold air

use.description: Decoration or play
use.link_to_grasp: holding the neck secures the balloon for movement
use.physical_constraints: thin material may tear if grip is too high

robustness.assumptions: balloon is dry and lying flat
robustness.uncertainty_handling: use soft silicone gripper to prevent puncturing the latex

object_identification.name: uninflated orange balloon
object_identification.attributes: small, orange, latex, deformable, flat body with a distinct rolled neck and rim

grasp.type: precision pinch grasp on the neck/rim
grasp.contact_zone: The two opposing pads of a two-fingered gripper will contact the edges of the thick rolled neck of the balloon.
grasp.accessibility: High, object is flat on a clear surface with significant clearance around the neck.

planning.approach: vertical down approach (top-down) centered on the balloon's neck, avoiding contact with the flat body. A sliding grasp under the body might be needed if the surface has high friction, but precision on the neck is more reliable for handling.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Gripper should be oriented with fingertips perpendicular to the plane of the balloon on the table, aligning the opening between fingers with the neck cylinder.
planning.grasp_contact: Lower the gripper until fingertips just contact the surface. Slowly close the gripper, allowing the tips to slide slightly outward and then pinch the thickened neck from the sides.
planning.lift_stabilization: Slow, upward acceleration. The soft material conformability to the fingertips provides friction. Minimize sideways motion until well clear of the table.

use.description: inflation on a nozzle
use.link_to_grasp: The pinch grasp on the neck allows the end-effector to bring the balloon's opening directly to a standard inflation nozzle. The rest of the balloon body is free to unfold.
use.physical_constraints: Requires precise nozzle alignment to the neck opening.

use.description: sorting into a bin or bag
use.link_to_grasp: The grasp is sufficiently secure to transport the light object over a distance for sorting. Dropping into a container is straightforward upon finger opening.
use.physical_constraints: Requires defining target boundaries for sorting.

robustness.assumptions: Assumes the rubber surface is dry and reasonable traction can be achieved via fingertip friction. Assumes the object isn't sticking intensely to the table material.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Use tactile feedback from fingertip sensors to detect slip during claw closure or lifting. High visual uncertainty might necessitate a larger initial grasp opening, followed by a 'scooping' motion under the body.

object_identification.name: orange latex balloon
object_identification.attributes: smooth surface, flexible material, elongated teardrop shape, translucent, rigid neck opening

grasp.contact_zone: neck opening and lower body
grasp.accessibility: moderate (requires stable approach due to flexibility)

planning.approach: vertical from above, aligned with balloon’s long axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper fingers parallel to balloon’s neck, slightly angled inward
planning.grasp_contact: soft fingertips or suction pad on neck, light pinch on lower body
planning.lift_stabilization: gentle upward force with minimal rotation to avoid deformation

use.description: decorative floating element for indoor events or children’s parties
use.link_to_grasp: pinch and lift gently to avoid tearing the neck during transport
use.physical_constraints: avoid sharp edges or surfaces that may puncture; handle with dry hands

use.description: visual marker or lightweight tool for experiments (e.g., buoyancy tests)
use.link_to_grasp: secure grasp around neck to prevent accidental release during experiments
use.physical_constraints: ensure no obstructions near the balloon’s path; avoid high-pressure environments

robustness.assumptions: balloon is inflated but not overfilled, dry surface, no dust/debris on contact zones
robustness.uncertainty_handling: use force control to adapt to slight shape variations; incorporate visual feedback for neck detection

object_identification.name: deflated orange latex balloon
object_identification.attributes: bright orange thin flexible latex, fully deflated into an elongated compact shape, has a hollow open neck, lightweight, smooth matte material

grasp.contact_zone: the thick hollow neck of the balloon, 5mm from the open neck end to avoid slippage
grasp.accessibility: fully unobstructed, positioned centrally on the table with no surrounding objects blocking access to any part of the balloon

planning.approach: approach from directly above the table surface, aligning the gripper with the balloon's longitudinal axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper fingers oriented parallel to the balloon's neck, positioned 1cm above the contact point to lower slowly onto the target
planning.grasp_contact: close the two-finger gripper evenly on the neck, applying minimal controlled force to secure the lightweight object without puncturing or squeezing through the thin latex
planning.lift_stabilization: lift the balloon 15cm above the table slowly, keeping the gripper level to prevent the deflated body from swinging, maintain consistent low grasp force to avoid dropping the object

use.description: inflate the balloon to use as a floating decorative ornament for parties or events
use.link_to_grasp: the same precision pinch grasp on the neck can hold the neck open while an air pump inserts air to inflate the balloon fully
use.physical_constraints: the latex can only stretch to a maximum inflation volume before popping, grasp force must stay low after inflation to avoid puncturing the thin expanded material

use.description: use the deflated balloon as a non-slip grip cover for small handheld craft tools
use.link_to_grasp: after grasping the balloon's neck, slide the open neck over the end of a small tool handle, maintaining the pinch grasp until the balloon is secured tightly onto the tool
use.physical_constraints: only fits tool handles with a diameter smaller than the latex neck's maximum stretch capacity, cannot support heavy loads that would tear the thin material

robustness.assumptions: the balloon is fully intact with no pre-existing punctures, the table surface is flat and stable, the robotic gripper has fine motor control for small object manipulation
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if the initial neck grasp slips, reposition the gripper to pinch the thickest part of the deflated balloon body to lift and reorient it for a more secure grasp, use visual edge detection to confirm the neck's location if low light impairs clear identification

object_identification.name: blue energy drink can
object_identification.attributes: Small cylindrical aluminum beverage can with blue label, pull-tab top, approximately 250 mL, standing upright on a table, likely a slim energy drink can.

grasp.type: precision power grasp with two to three fingers and thumb
grasp.contact_zone: lateral cylindrical surface around the mid-body, avoiding the top rim and pull-tab
grasp.accessibility: Highly accessible from all sides when unobstructed; stable upright pose on a flat table allows a straight-side approach and easy lift

planning.approach: Move the gripper to the side of the can with a gentle horizontal approach aligned to the can’s center height, stopping just outside the can diameter before closing.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Keep the gripper jaws parallel to the can axis or slightly angled to match the cylindrical surface, with the opening centered at mid-height.
planning.grasp_contact: Close fingers evenly on the can’s sidewall to create frictional contact without deforming the thin aluminum; avoid the top lid and bottom edge.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift vertically with low acceleration, maintain symmetric squeeze force, and keep the can upright to prevent slipping, tipping, or denting.

use.description: Transport the drink can from the table to a serving or storage location.
use.link_to_grasp: The side grasp provides enough friction and stability for controlled relocation of a lightweight cylindrical object.
use.physical_constraints: Thin aluminum can be dented by excessive force; contents may shift, so lifting should be smooth and upright.

use.description: Place the can into a cup holder, tray slot, or recycling bin.
use.link_to_grasp: A centered side grasp allows precise positioning and release after transport.
use.physical_constraints: Requires careful alignment with the container opening and enough clearance for the can’s diameter and height.

robustness.assumptions: The can is closed, intact, and roughly full or partially full; the surface is dry enough to provide friction; the table is level.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If the pose or fill state is uncertain, use a lower-force adaptive grasp, verify contact with force feedback, and re-center if slip is detected.

object_identification.name: blue energy drink can
object_identification.attributes: cylindrical aluminum container, side-lying on table, blue label with white text, 250ml size, irregular textured pattern on label

grasp.contact_zone: upper-middle lateral surface of the cylinder
grasp.accessibility: high, fully exposed on flat wooden surface with no occlusions

planning.approach: vertical approach from positive Z axis perpendicular to table
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: robot arm vertical, fingers extended and parallel to Z axis
planning.grasp_contact: fingers contact the curved side wall of the cylinder simultaneously
planning.lift_stabilization: lift strictly along Z axis; object will naturally rotate to upright standing position due to center of mass

use.description: consuming the beverage by bringing to mouth
use.link_to_grasp: requires lifting fully upright and orienting pull-tab towards user
use.physical_constraints: must prevent spillage, requires careful control of liquid dynamics

use.description: disposing in recycling bin
use.link_to_grasp: allows easy transport and manual opening for recycling
use.physical_constraints: can be dropped or inverted without leaking if empty, though currently full

robustness.assumptions: can is sealed, rigid metal cylinder resting flatly on table
robustness.uncertainty_handling: plan accounts for slip due to smooth aluminum surface by using compliant grip or moderate finger force

object_identification.name: Standard V Blue energy drink can
object_identification.attributes: cylindrical shape, 250ml capacity, thin metal (aluminum) wall, blue color with a dark 'V' logo, smooth but with vertical textured ridges often found on these specific slim cans.

grasp.contact_zone: Opposing large palm area and finger pads wrapping around the central cylindrical body of the can.
grasp.accessibility: High. The object is in the open on a flat table, allowing an unobstructed approach from nearly any side angle.

planning.approach: Horizontal parallel jaw gripper approach or enveloping multi-finger hand approach, targeting the center of mass.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Rotate end-effector so parallel jaws are vertical to standard orientation, ready to close on sides.
planning.grasp_contact: Simultaneous closure of gripper fingers, applying uniform inwards radial force to the can wall.
planning.lift_stabilization: Verify stable contact force. Apply a small upward vertical lift motion. Adjust force if slipping detected while maintaining force to prevent damage.

use.description: Transporting the can from the table to a cup or designated location for consumption (if opened) or storage.
use.link_to_grasp: The central cylindrical grasp is fundamental for lift and stable transport.
use.physical_constraints: Must maintain can upright to prevent contents from spilling if open.

use.description: Holding the can steady to allow a specialized opening tool or secondary manipulator to actuate the pull-tab.
use.link_to_grasp: The firm grip provides counter-force against the torque and pull forces needed to lift the tab.
use.physical_constraints: Requires high frictional force to prevent rotation of the can around its vertical axis during opening.

robustness.assumptions: The can is on a stable, flat surface. The rigid body model of a full or non-empty can holds sufficiently for standard gripper force.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: In case of slippage, use force sensors to detect and increase grasp force. For misaligned approach, use visual servoing to correct the hand vector.

object_identification.name: energy_drink_can
object_identification.attributes: cylindrical_shape, smooth_surface, blue_color, aluminum_material, curved_top, pull_tab_closure, 250ml_capacity

grasp.contact_zone: mid_cylinder_and_top_rim
grasp.accessibility: front_and_side_visible

planning.approach: vertical_alignment_from_above
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper_fingers_parallel_to_can_axis
planning.grasp_contact: finger_pads_on_can_body_and_opposing_finger_on_top_rim
planning.lift_stabilization: controlled_lift_with_rotational_stabilization_around_center

use.description: consumption_as_energy_boost_drink
use.link_to_grasp: requires_stable_hold_to_open_tab_and_pour_content
use.physical_constraints: can_must_be_held_upright_to_prevent_spillage_and_tab_must_be_accessible_for_opening

use.description: recycling_processing
use.link_to_grasp: requires_secure_grasp_for_transport_to_recycling_bin
use.physical_constraints: must_avoid_damage_to_can_surface_during_transport_to_maintain_recycling_integrity

robustness.assumptions: can_is_unopened_and_stable_on_surface_with_minimal_obstacles
robustness.uncertainty_handling: visual_depth_sensing_for_contact_zone_detection_and_force_control_for_gentle_closure

object_identification.name: V Blue guarana energy drink aluminum can
object_identification.attributes: 250mL cylindrical aluminum beverage can, glossy blue exterior with printed branding, unopened in the first image, opened pull tab in the second image, resting on a wooden office table

grasp.contact_zone: mid-lateral sides of the can, 1/3 up from the base to avoid the top rim, gripping the smooth aluminum surface evenly
grasp.accessibility: fully accessible, no surrounding obstacles blocking approach, sits on an open table with 360-degree horizontal clearance

planning.approach: approach the can from directly above, descending vertically toward the table to align the gripper with the can's central vertical axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper jaws oriented parallel to the can's circular cross-section, opened wide enough to clear the can's diameter before closing
planning.grasp_contact: close gripper jaws evenly on the left and right mid-lateral surfaces, apply sufficient force to prevent slippage without deforming the thin aluminum can
planning.lift_stabilization: lift the can vertically 15cm above the table, pause to confirm the can is stable with no lateral shifting before initiating any horizontal movement

use.description: Serve the can to a person to consume the remaining energy drink
use.link_to_grasp: The same cylindrical grasp used to lift the can can position it stably within a user's reach on a nearby surface
use.physical_constraints: Limit tilting the can to less than 10 degrees during transport to avoid spilling liquid from the opened pull tab, maintain consistent grip force to prevent dropping

use.description: Dispose of the empty can in a recycling bin
use.link_to_grasp: The cylindrical grasp supports carrying the can from the table to the recycling bin, with enough control to place the can into the bin opening without issues
use.physical_constraints: Avoid applying excess grip force that would crush the aluminum can, ensure no residual liquid remains to prevent spillage during transport to the recycling bin

robustness.assumptions: Assume the can's aluminum surface is dry and non-slippery, the can's structure is intact with no dents that interfere with grasping, and any remaining liquid does not shift enough to unbalance the can during movement
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If initial grasp detects slippage, reposition the gripper to a wider contact area and adjust grip force slightly; if unforeseen obstacles block the top approach, switch to a side-approach to grasp the can from a clear horizontal direction

object_identification.name: USB flash drive
object_identification.attributes: Small rectangular portable storage device with a metal USB-A connector on one end, a white plastic body, printed text on the корпус, and a compact form factor suitable for precision pinching.

grasp.contact_zone: Gripper fingertips should contact the plastic body near the center or rear portion, avoiding the metal connector and avoiding the capped end if present.
grasp.accessibility: Highly accessible on a flat tabletop, with ample clearance around the object; the small size requires careful approach and fine alignment.

planning.approach: Approach from above with the gripper open, align the fingers parallel to the long axis of the drive, and descend slowly to the body center.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Orient the gripper so the fingertips straddle the widest faces of the body, with the USB connector pointing away from the grasping fingers to reduce slip and avoid interference.
planning.grasp_contact: Close the fingers gently until firm contact is made on the plastic shell, applying enough force to secure the drive without crushing or rotating it.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift vertically a short distance, then pause to verify no slip or tilt; if needed, regrasp slightly closer to the center of mass before transport.

use.description: Transfer data between a robot workstation and another computer.
use.link_to_grasp: A stable pinch grasp allows the robot to pick up the drive, insert it, and remove it cleanly.
use.physical_constraints: Small size, insertion alignment tolerance, and fragile connector require accurate pose control.

use.description: Store or retrieve logs, maps, or calibration files for maintenance tasks.
use.link_to_grasp: The same secure grasp supports picking the device from a desk and placing it into a USB port or storage tray.
use.physical_constraints: Requires secure retention during motion and careful handling to avoid bending the connector.

robustness.assumptions: The object is a standard USB flash drive resting freely on a flat surface and is not attached to a lanyard or partially inserted into another device.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If the exact orientation is uncertain, the robot should use visual servoing to refine alignment, grasp the thickest body section rather than the connector, and retry with a smaller closing force if slip is detected.

object_identification.name: USB flash drive
object_identification.attributes: Small white and grey rectangular storage device with a USB connector exposed, lying on a light wood-grain desk.

grasp.contact_zone: Top surfaces of the white plastic body of the USB drive
grasp.accessibility: Fully accessible from above on the flat desk surface

planning.approach: Approach from top-down to avoid collision with the nearby office chair.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Align the gripper parallel to the USB drive's long axis for stability.
planning.grasp_contact: Make gentle contact on both sides of the white body to minimize torque.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift slowly to avoid scratching the table and adjust orientation to hold the USB securely by its main body.

use.description: Data transfer: Plugging into a computer to copy files.
use.link_to_grasp: A standard precision grasp by the body is required to maintain a low profile for inserting the connector.
use.physical_constraints: Must expose the connector without triggering the safety switch (if applicable).

use.description: Storage: Attaching it to a lanyard for portability.
use.link_to_grasp: Grasping the body firmly allows a user to thread a lanyard through the hole (if any) or just hold it securely.
use.physical_constraints: The connector must be protected during movement.

robustness.assumptions: The USB drive is solid plastic and not loose enough to roll significantly.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Control approach speed and force to accommodate slight variations in object size and material.

object_identification.name: USB flash drive
object_identification.attributes: small, rigid, light, cylindrical white/blue body, metallic silver USB-C connector visible, lay flat on wooden surface.

grasp.contact_zone: The gripper fingers should contact the wide, flat sides of the white/blue plastic body, parallel to the surface.
grasp.accessibility: High. The object is small but fully exposed on a flat table.

planning.approach: From directly above (top-down), with the gripper fully open.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Align the gripper fingers to be parallel with the long axis of the USB body, perpendicular to the flat sides of the body.
planning.grasp_contact: Lower the gripper and gently close the fingers on the flat plastic surfaces, applying just enough force to secure it without slipping or crushing.
planning.lift_stabilization: Perform a steady vertical lift to clear the surface before transferring.

use.description: Insert into portable electronic device (e.g., tablet, phone) port.
use.link_to_grasp: The current side grisp allows for direct, 6-DOF controlled linear insertion of the metal USB-C part into a mating port.
use.physical_constraints: Small object manipulation requirements; requires high precision for alignment and low insertion force control.

use.description: Place into a storage slot or carry pouch.
use.link_to_grasp: Place or drop into a designated small compartment.
use.physical_constraints: Less precise placement required than port insertion, but requires releasing above a narrow slot or tray.

robustness.assumptions: Accurate 3D position (pose estimation) and knowledge of dimensions.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Use close-range visual servoing or tactile feedback to correct for misalignments; use compliant control for port insertion if needed.

object_identification.name: USB flash drive
object_identification.attributes: small rectangular plastic casing, metallic USB connector, smooth surface, dimensions approximately 50x15x2mm, lightweight, non-slip texture on connector

grasp.contact_zone: metallic USB connector and adjacent plastic body
grasp.accessibility: limited due to small size and connector orientation

planning.approach: top-down or slight angle to avoid blocking connector
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: fingers aligned parallel to USB connector, gripper fingers slightly open
planning.grasp_contact: two fingers on opposite sides of the connector, thumb stabilizing the plastic body
planning.lift_stabilization: gentle upward force with rotational stabilization to prevent connector misalignment

use.description: Data transfer between computers or devices via USB ports
use.link_to_grasp: grasp ensures connector alignment for plugging into a USB port
use.physical_constraints: connector must be clean and free of debris; grip must avoid damaging the plastic casing

use.description: Portable storage device for backup and archival of digital files
use.link_to_grasp: secure grasp allows handling without dropping or bending connector
use.physical_constraints: avoid excessive force to prevent connector bending or casing cracks

robustness.assumptions: object is stationary, USB connector is undamaged, lighting and surface are uniform for detection
robustness.uncertainty_handling: use force control to avoid slippage; implement visual feedback for connector alignment; apply suction or textured gripper pads if friction is insufficient

object_identification.name: USB flash drive
object_identification.attributes: Small rectangular portable data storage device, white plastic body with metal USB connector, gray end cap, smooth surface, resting flat on an open wooden table

grasp.contact_zone: Opposite smooth plastic side surfaces of the USB drive, avoiding the metal connector and gray end cap to prevent damage
grasp.accessibility: Fully unobstructed, visible and reachable from all sides with no surrounding objects blocking access

planning.approach: Move the robotic end-effector vertically down toward the table, aligning the gripper directly over the center of the USB drive
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Align the gripper's pinch axis parallel to the long axis of the USB drive, so the two gripper fingers can wrap evenly around the drive's narrow sides
planning.grasp_contact: Close the gripper fingers to apply gentle, uniform pressure on the drive's side surfaces, ensuring a secure hold without damaging the plastic casing
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift the USB drive straight upward 10cm above the table, maintaining a level orientation to prevent slipping, and confirm the grasp is secure before initiating any lateral movement

use.description: Transfer and store digital files by inserting the USB drive into a computer's USB port
use.link_to_grasp: The same pinch grasp is maintained to align the metal USB connector with the target port, then the gripper releases once the drive is fully inserted
use.physical_constraints: Connector must be aligned within 1mm of the USB port opening to avoid damage; gripper must release completely to not obstruct the port after insertion

use.description: Securely store the USB drive by placing it into a dedicated protective storage case slot
use.link_to_grasp: The same pinch grasp is used to position the drive into the case's foam slot, then the gripper releases once the drive is properly seated
use.physical_constraints: Drive must be oriented correctly to fit the case's slot; low placement force is required to avoid damaging the drive or the case's interior

robustness.assumptions: The USB drive is stationary on a flat, stable table; its plastic body is rigid enough to withstand pinch grasp pressure; no slippery coating is present on the drive's surface
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Use visual feedback from on-board cameras to reposition the gripper if initial alignment is incorrect; if the drive slips during lift, lower it back to the table, re-grasp with slightly increased pressure, and reconfirm the hold before lifting again

object_identification.name: whiteboard eraser
object_identification.attributes: Small rectangular eraser with a wooden body and dark felt or foam wiping surface, low profile, lightweight, and sized for hand-held cleaning of a whiteboard.

grasp.type: precision side grasp or pinch grasp
grasp.contact_zone: Grasp the wooden side faces near the center of the block, avoiding the dark wiping surface so it remains clean and functional.
grasp.accessibility: Highly accessible on the tabletop with clear open space around it; the object lies flat and can be approached from above or slightly from the side.

planning.approach: Move the gripper above the eraser along the short axis, align with the rectangular body, and descend vertically with slow speed.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Orient the gripper jaws parallel to the long sides of the eraser so the contacts land on opposite wooden faces.
planning.grasp_contact: Close the fingers gently until stable lateral contact is made on the wooden sides, applying enough force to prevent slipping without compressing the wiping surface.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift straight up a short distance, keep the gripper level, and monitor for rotation or slip because the object is small and lightweight.

use.description: Erase marker writing from a whiteboard.
use.link_to_grasp: The side grasp keeps the wiping face unobstructed so the robot can press the felt side onto the board surface.
use.physical_constraints: Requires controlled contact force, flat alignment with the board, and enough friction to sweep across the surface without dropping.

use.description: Move or store the eraser in a holder or cleaning station.
use.link_to_grasp: A stable side grasp allows transport without touching the dirty wiping surface and enables precise placement.
use.physical_constraints: Needs low-mass handling, careful orientation control, and collision-free motion in cluttered desk or board areas.

robustness.assumptions: The object is a standard whiteboard eraser resting stably on a flat table with no strong adhesion or attachment.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If pose is uncertain, use vision to estimate the rectangular orientation, approach from above, and increase grasp tolerance slightly while keeping contact on the wooden sides rather than the wiping face.

object_identification.name: Wooden Eraser
object_identification.attributes: Small rectangular object consisting of a light-colored wooden handle block and a dark grey/black eraser block adhered to one side. The wooden grain is visible on the handle sides.

grasp.contact_zone: The narrow lateral sides (long edges) of the wooden block or the short ends
grasp.accessibility: High - The object is centrally located and lying flat on a surface.

planning.approach: Approach from above the object to identify orientation
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Align the motion direction of the robot gripper fingers parallel to the long axis of the eraser
planning.grasp_contact: Close fingers to clamp the sides firmly, wrapping around the corners
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift vertically, ensuring the friction coefficient of the wooden sides is sufficient to hold it without slipping.

use.description: Erasing marks from a whiteboard or blackboard
use.link_to_grasp: The firm pinch allows for applying lateral pressure against the board surface while rubbing
use.physical_constraints: The eraser block must be oriented flat against the board; grip must not rotate the block during rubbing.

use.description: Scraping glue or paint residue from edges
use.link_to_grasp: The handle provides a good lever arm for pushing with the rough side of the eraser
use.physical_constraints: Requires a side-grasp to push the eraser tip without slipping forward.

robustness.assumptions: The table surface is frictionless or smooth under the eraser; the eraser is rigid.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: If the gripper slips, the robot should increase closing force or re-grasp at the other end which may have a different texture.

object_identification.name: whiteboard eraser
object_identification.attributes: rectangular prism, wooden block body with dark felt erasing surface, currently resting felt-side down on a wooden desk

grasp.contact_zone: the two long vertical sides of the wooden block body
grasp.accessibility: high, as the long sides are fully exposed since it’s resting flat

planning.approach: vertical top-down approach centered over the object with the gripper open and oriented perpendicular to the object's long axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: aligning the gripper jaw pads parallel to the long vertical faces of the wooden block
planning.grasp_contact: lowering the gripper until pads make initial contact with the lower half of the sides, then closing the jaws to apply a secure, regulated force
planning.lift_stabilization: lifting the object straight up along the vertical axis while monitoring for changes in gripping force to detect slip

use.description: cleaning a portion of a whiteboard surface
use.link_to_grasp: the initial grasp from the sides keeps all faces of the eraser usable. Reorientation would be needed to press the felt side to the board.
use.physical_constraints: requires a subsequent in-hand manipulation or robotic arm maneuver to orient the felt surface parallel and make contact with the vertical/titled whiteboard plane

use.description: moving or repositioning the eraser to a new location on a desk or whiteboard ledge
use.link_to_grasp: the chosen grasp is stable and ideal for pick-and-place tasks without modifying the object's orientation
use.physical_constraints: requires simple XY plane translation after the vertical lift, followed by a controlled descent and release onto the new surface

robustness.assumptions: the object is rigid and its mass distribution is uniform, preventing rotation or center-of-gravity displacement during lifting
robustness.uncertainty_handling: using visual servoing to correct the object's exact center-of-grasp and employing force feedback to verify successful tactile contact before initiating a vertical lift

object_identification.name: whiteboard eraser
object_identification.attributes: rectangular prism shape, soft rubber material, textured surface, light gray color, flat edges, length approximately 7-8 cm, width approximately 2-3 cm, thickness approximately 1-2 cm

grasp.contact_zone: flat side edges and rubber surface
grasp.accessibility: moderate (requires alignment with gripper fingers or suction pad)

planning.approach: vertical descent from above, aligned with eraser’s long axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper fingers parallel to eraser’s length, slightly tilted for stable contact
planning.grasp_contact: fingers press on flat sides with slight inward force along the eraser’s length
planning.lift_stabilization: gentle upward lift with slight rotation to prevent slippage

use.description: remove chalk or marker residue from whiteboards or dry-erase surfaces
use.link_to_grasp: grasp ensures firm contact for effective scrubbing motion
use.physical_constraints: must avoid sharp edges to prevent surface damage; requires sufficient grip force to prevent slippage during scrubbing

use.description: erase temporary markers or sticky notes on glass or smooth surfaces
use.link_to_grasp: flat surface contact allows even pressure distribution for cleaning
use.physical_constraints: requires controlled force to avoid scratching surfaces; grip must secure eraser during sideways motion

robustness.assumptions: eraser is clean and dry, no obstructions on whiteboard surface, gripper has sufficient force and precision
robustness.uncertainty_handling: use vision feedback to adjust approach angle if eraser is misaligned; apply variable grip force based on surface friction detection

object_identification.name: whiteboard eraser
object_identification.attributes: small rectangular object, with a rigid light wood base and a dark soft felt cleaning surface, lightweight, rests flat on flat work surfaces, felt side is the functional erasing surface, wood side provides structural support

grasp.contact_zone: the solid wood side edges of the eraser, avoiding the soft felt cleaning surface to prevent contamination or compression of the felt
grasp.accessibility: fully unobstructed, placed on an open table surface with no surrounding objects blocking access from any approach direction

planning.approach: move the robot gripper directly above the eraser, aligned to straddle the long axis of the rectangular eraser
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: orient gripper jaws parallel to the short ends of the eraser, open the jaws wider than the eraser's width to lower over the object without collision
planning.grasp_contact: close gripper jaws to apply gentle, firm pressure on the rigid wood side faces of the eraser, ensuring the felt surface is not squeezed or damaged
planning.lift_stabilization: lift the eraser 10-15cm straight upward from the table to confirm stable grasp with no slippage before moving to the target use location

use.description: erase dry-erase ink markings from a whiteboard by wiping the felt surface across the whiteboard surface
use.link_to_grasp: retain the original pinch grasp, orient the eraser to bring the felt side flush against the whiteboard surface for cleaning
use.physical_constraints: maintain light consistent downward pressure during wiping, avoid excessive force that could break the grasp or damage the whiteboard surface

use.description: wipe dry dust and light smudges off polished wood or glass table surfaces using the soft felt side
use.link_to_grasp: keep the same pinch grasp to maintain control, position the felt surface against the dirty table surface to clean
use.physical_constraints: only use on dry non-porous surfaces, avoid exposure to moisture that would warp the wood base or ruin the felt cleaning material

robustness.assumptions: the eraser is uniformly weighted, the wood base is rigid enough to withstand standard grasp force, the object is lightweight enough to avoid slippage during movement
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if initial grasp slippage is detected, re-center the gripper on the eraser's long axis, adjust grasp force slightly to secure the object without over-compressing the felt core

object_identification.name: wooden stirrer stick
object_identification.attributes: small, thin, flat disposable wooden stick with rounded ends, light beige color, rigid but fragile, lying flat on a tabletop

grasp.contact_zone: grip the stick near one end or around the center along the long edges, avoiding the rounded tips for better control
grasp.accessibility: high accessibility because the object is isolated on an open flat surface and fully visible, but low thickness requires accurate fingertip placement

planning.approach: move the gripper above the stick with a top-down approach, align with the stick's long axis, and descend slowly to avoid pushing it away
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: orient the gripper parallel to the stick length with jaws centered over the selected grasp point and slightly open to the stick thickness
planning.grasp_contact: close fingertips gently on opposite long edges with enough force to prevent slip but not so much that the stick bends or cracks
planning.lift_stabilization: lift vertically a short distance while keeping the stick horizontal, then stabilize with low-speed motion to prevent rotation due to its light mass and long shape

use.description: stir a beverage such as coffee, tea, or a mixed drink
use.link_to_grasp: the pinch grasp provides control of the slim body so the robot can insert the stick into a cup and move it through the liquid
use.physical_constraints: must maintain hygiene, avoid splintering, and keep the stick sufficiently rigid under stirring forces

use.description: mix small quantities of paint, glue, or laboratory liquid in a container
use.link_to_grasp: a controlled grasp near one end allows the robot to use the opposite end as the mixing tip while keeping the hand clear of the material
use.physical_constraints: the stick is disposable and lightweight, so it is suitable only for low-torque mixing tasks and may weaken if soaked too long

robustness.assumptions: the object is a standard disposable wooden stirrer on a clear tabletop with no occlusion and no nearby obstacles
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if the pose estimate is uncertain, the robot should re-image from a closer view, choose the midsection for maximum grasp margin, and use compliant low-force closure to compensate for the thin geometry

object_identification.name: wooden stirrer
object_identification.attributes: slender, tapered, fine-textured material, rough surface

grasp.contact_zone: central section
grasp.accessibility: easily accessible from above

planning.approach: approach from above and slightly from the side
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper oriented vertically
planning.grasp_contact: gripper touches stirrer
planning.lift_stabilization: lift slowly and gently

use.description: stirring a drink
use.link_to_grasp: pinch grasp
use.physical_constraints: light weight and limited length

use.description: testing material strength
use.link_to_grasp: pinch grasp
use.physical_constraints: brittle fracture under higher force

robustness.assumptions: stirrer is at the same distance as in the image and is horizontal on a flat surface
robustness.uncertainty_handling: slightly close the gripper if the stirrer slides or slips

object_identification.name: wooden coffee stirrer
object_identification.attributes: lightweight, thin, flat, rectangular, light brown, wood, rough texture

grasp.contact_zone: The central section of the long edges, approximately one-third of the way from either end.
grasp.accessibility: High. The object is isolated on a flat surface with wide open workspace on all sides, allowing for arbitrary parallel gripper orientations.

planning.approach: Vertical straight-down approach towards the center of the stirrer's longest axis.
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: Align the parallel jaws of the gripper perpendicular to the long axis of the stirrer, with a wide enough gap to clear the entire object width.
planning.grasp_contact: Lower the gripper until the fingertips are just above the table surface, then simultaneously close both jaws to apply gentle clamping force on the thin sides.
planning.lift_stabilization: Lift the stirrer straight up at a slow, controlled speed to avoid slippage or tilting.

use.description: stirring a liquid, such as coffee, in a cup to mix ingredients
use.link_to_grasp: This grasp configuration holds the stirrer at the top, leaving most of its length free for insertion into a container of liquid for stirring.
use.physical_constraints: Limited force application: pushing down or rotating too quickly can cause the stirrer to flex and potentially break. The grasp point should be high enough not to interfere with the stirring container.

use.description: as a makeshift lever for light-duty lifting of small objects
use.link_to_grasp: The precise pinch grasp at the center provides good balanced leverage when working under lightweight items.
use.physical_constraints: The thin wood is not designed for significant load-bearing. This use is only feasible for extremely lightweight objects and small tilting forces.

robustness.assumptions: The object is assumed to be dry and clean, preventing slipperiness. The table surface is assumed to be flat and stable. Its mass is assumed to be negligible.
robustness.uncertainty_handling: Small rotational misalignment of the jaws is tolerated by the relatively wide grasping area. Slow grasping and lifting speeds minimize dynamic uncertainties. The roughness of the wood helps prevent rotational slip.

object_identification.name: stirring spoon
object_identification.attributes: long slender handle with a flat, slightly curved spatula end; lightweight; smooth surface; metallic or plastic material; approximately 20-30 cm length

grasp.contact_zone: flat spatula end and midsection of handle
grasp.accessibility: high (exposed, no occlusion)

planning.approach: top-down alignment parallel to handle axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper fingers parallel to spatula surface, slightly tilted for stable insertion
planning.grasp_contact: fingers clamp spatula edge while thumb pad contacts handle midsection
planning.lift_stabilization: firm parallel grip with slight downward force to prevent slipping

use.description: mixing liquids in a container (e.g., coffee stirrer)
use.link_to_grasp: flat spatula end ensures even distribution of liquid; handle grip provides controlled rotation
use.physical_constraints: object must be inserted perpendicular to container surface; handle must remain clear of container edges

use.description: scraping or spreading viscous substances (e.g., frosting, paint)
use.link_to_grasp: flat spatula end designed for surface contact; grip allows directional control
use.physical_constraints: surface must be flat and non-porous; handle must remain unobstructed for maneuverability

robustness.assumptions: object is clean and dry; gripper fingers are soft-tipped; lighting ensures accurate depth perception
robustness.uncertainty_handling: adaptive force control for varying material stiffness; visual feedback for handle alignment correction

object_identification.name: wooden drink stirrer
object_identification.attributes: thin, flat, lightweight small wooden stick, smooth surface, rounded ends, low structural strength, positioned flat on a horizontal unobstructed surface

grasp.contact_zone: the middle third section of the stirrer, leaving both ends free for functional use
grasp.accessibility: fully accessible, no surrounding obstacles, can be approached from any vertical angle above the table surface

planning.approach: descend vertically from directly above the stirrer, aligning gripper fingers parallel to the stirrer's long axis
planning.pre_grasp_orientation: gripper oriented to span the thin width of the stirrer, positioned 2cm above the stirrer's midpoint with fingers open wide enough to clear the stick's width
planning.grasp_contact: slowly close gripper fingers to apply light, firm pressure on the midsection, sufficient to secure the lightweight stick without splintering or cracking the wood
planning.lift_stabilization: lift the stirrer 15cm vertically above the table, pause briefly to confirm grasp stability, maintain a level orientation to prevent slipping during subsequent movement

use.description: Stir hot or cold consumable beverages to mix in sugar, creamer, or flavor additives
use.link_to_grasp: retain the midsection precision pinch grasp, orient one free clean end of the stirrer to insert into the drink and perform circular stirring motions
use.physical_constraints: only use an unused, clean end for contact with consumable liquids, avoid excessive force that could snap the thin wooden stick

use.description: Spread small portions of soft substances like craft glue, cream cheese, or paint onto flat surfaces for food prep or DIY tasks
use.link_to_grasp: keep the midsection pinch grasp, orient the flat wide end of the stirrer to contact and spread soft substances in steady wiping motions
use.physical_constraints: limit applied force to avoid bending or breaking the lightweight wood, only use on low-viscosity soft substances that do not require heavy spreading pressure

robustness.assumptions: the stirrer is structurally intact with no pre-existing cracks, it is stationary on the table, the gripper can detect light contact pressure to avoid damaging the wood
robustness.uncertainty_handling: if initial grasp misalignment is detected, reposition the gripper to center on the stirrer's midpoint; if the stirrer shifts during grasp, release and re-approach to resecure the stick before lifting
